data.title: U.S. LNG plants running near full capacity, limiting potential to replace Qatar disruptions
data.description:
Even as the world's largest liquefied natural gas producer, the U.S. has little spare capacity to quickly raise LNG output to offset lost supply after Qatar stopped shipments, Reuters reported Wednesday.

The U.S. exports nearly 19B cf/day of natural gas that is converted into LNG, nearly double the 10B cf/day Qatar removed from the market after the wave of attacks on Iran, but domestic export plants are running nearly full tilt with most cargoes locked into long‑term contracts.

Top U.S. exporter Cheniere Energy (LNG) sold 46M metric tons of LNG last year and on Tuesday was drawing more than 7B cf/day of feed gas for its two Gulf Coast terminals; the company recently began production from Train 5 of its Stage 3 ⁠expansion at Corpus Christi, the unit - with capacity to produce a relatively small 1.5M tons/year - is expected to take about a month to reach full output, most of which has been contracted out.

Second-ranked U.S. producer Venture Global (VG) has the most flexibility to help in ​the short term because it is selling as much as 4B cf/day of commissioning volumes from its Plaquemines plant in Louisiana on the spot market, which allows the company more room to redirect cargoes, CEO Mike Sabel said this week on an earnings conference call; Plaquemines will be capable of producing 35M tons/year when fully online.

The Golden Pass LNG project, a joint venture between QatarEnergy and Exxon ‌Mobil (XOM), is expected to ⁠begin initial production this month, with demand for gas to be converted into LNG expected to reach ~800M cf/day for the first 6M tons/year plant.

"With the U.S. already exporting LNG at effectively maximum capacity, the war cannot materially increase physical demand for U.S. gas in the short‑ to medium‑term," EBW Analytics said in a note.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Wednesday: Crypto stocks, WIX, MRNA, and more
data.description:
Stock futures inched higher Wednesday morning as investors assessed escalating Middle East conflict, labor data for interest rate insights, and grappled with surging oil prices stoking inflation fears.

Here are some of Wednesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Strategy (MSTR) +10% - Cryptocurrency-linked stocks rallied alongside a rebound in Bitcoin, which climbed about 5% to $71.5K after a recent sell-off tied to escalating Middle East tensions. Other notable movers included MARA Holdings (MARA) up 5%, Coinbase (COIN) up 13%, Robinhood Markets (HOOD) +9%, Circle Internet Group (CRCL) +5%, Riot Platforms (RIOT) +8%, Galaxy Digital (GLXY) +12%, and Hut 8 (HUT) +12%.
  • Moderna (MRNA) +9% - Shares rose after reaching a $950M patent litigation settlement with Arbutus Biopharma ((ABUS)) and Genevant Sciences ((ROIV)), resolving disputes tied to its COVID-19 and RSV vaccines. The payment, due in Q3 and to be recorded as a Q1 charge, eliminates future royalty obligations and provides clarity across its infectious disease portfolio, including Spikevax, mRESVIA, mNEXSPIKE, and mCOMBRIAX. Following the settlement, Moderna expects to end the year with $4.5B–$5B in cash and equivalents. The company will appeal to the U.S. Federal Circuit, arguing that government-contractor immunity limits its liability; a favorable ruling would remove any additional payment, while an adverse decision could require up to another $1.3B within 90 days.
  • Wix.com (WIX) +9% - Shares surged despite mixed Q4 results, as investors focused on solid growth trends and forward guidance. Q4 bookings rose 15% Y/Y to $534.5M. The company expects consolidated revenue to grow at a mid-teens rate in Q1 and for FY2026, roughly in line with consensus estimates near 14% growth. Wix also guided free cash flow margin in the low- to mid-20% range for FY2026, noting variability tied to the rapid expansion of Base44, while its core business remains healthy and profitable with flat to expanding FCF margins. Additionally, Wix signed a private placement agreement with institutional investors led by Durable Capital Partners for up to $250M in gross proceeds, issuing units at a 5% discount to the current share price.
  • Ross Stores (ROST) +7% - Shares jumped after reporting strong Q4 results and issuing upbeat guidance, as solid holiday sales and stepped-up marketing helped offset tariff pressures and softer consumer sentiment. For Q1, the company expects comparable sales growth of 7% to 8%, well above estimates of about 3.7%, driving EPS of $1.60 to $1.67, up 11% Y/Y and around the $1.63 consensus. For FY2027, Ross forecasts same-store sales growth of 3% to 4% vs. roughly 3% expectations and EPS of $7.01 to $7.36 compared with $7.21 estimates. The company also hiked the dividend by 10%.

Biggest stock losers

  • Horizon Technology Finance (HRZN) -22% - Shares dipped after reporting net investment income and total investment income below expectations. Total investment income came in at $20.7M, missing the $23.9M estimate and declining from $26.3M in the prior quarter and $23.5M a year earlier. Although the company returned to portfolio growth in Q4, net asset value per share edged lower as distributions exceeded NII during the quarter, with management noting that reduced prepayment activity also weighed on results.
  • GitLab (GTLB) -10% - Shares slid despite a Q4 earnings beat and revenue growth of 23.2% Y/Y, as a softer-than-expected outlook weighed on sentiment. For Q1, the company guided revenue of $253M–$255M vs. the $256.7M consensus, with non-GAAP operating income of $32M–$34M and EPS of $0.20–$0.21, roughly in line with estimates. For FY2027, GitLab expects revenue of $1.099B–$1.118B, below the $1.13B consensus, along with non-GAAP operating income of $129M–$137M and EPS of $0.76–$0.80, well under the $1.03 forecast.
  • Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF) -7% - Shares tumbled as strong Q4 results, including record revenue, were overshadowed by softer-than-expected current-quarter guidance. The retailer forecasts Q1 EPS of $1.20–$1.30 on 1%–3% sales growth, or revenue of $1.11B–$1.13B, below consensus estimates of $1.45 per share on $1.16B in revenue. For FY2026, guidance was largely in line with expectations, with projected EPS of $10.20–$11.00 on 3%–5% sales growth, implying revenue of $5.43B–$5.53B, compared with estimates of $10.40 per share on $5.50B in sales.

data.title: High-quality fixed income currently looks ‘very attractive’ relative to other assets – PIMCO’s Mittal
data.description:
Current yields in high-quality fixed income look “very attractive on a risk-adjusted basis relative to other alternatives,” according to Mohit Mittal, PIMCO’s CIO of core strategies.

In an interview with CNBC, Mittal said yields in the 5% to 6% range offer superior value compared to equities and riskier credit markets, with the added benefit of liquidity that makes fixed income a compelling investment option.

The comments come amid unusual market dynamics where Treasury yields (US10Y), (US30Y), (US2Y) have risen despite the escalating U.S.-Iran conflict.

Typically, geopolitical tensions drive investors toward the safety of Treasuries, pushing prices up and yields down—but inflation concerns tied to rising energy prices have reversed that pattern. Mittal noted that despite rate cuts, fluctuating inflation data, and global conflict, the 10-year yield (US10Y) has remained range-bound between 4% and 4.20%.

Mittal identified the Iran escalation as one of three key themes impacting markets, explaining that it creates dual pressures.

“The near-term impact is on commodities, and then, potentially at least in the near term, on inflation,” he said, adding that “on a medium- to longer-term basis, it also has an impact on risk sentiment and could push yields lower.”

On monetary policy, PIMCO anticipates two interest rate cuts this year, with the possibility of more if economic growth slows. Mittal emphasized that “the hurdle to hike would be very, very high in this environment,” suggesting limited upside for rates and presenting opportunities for investors even if yields remain stable.

Beyond geopolitics, Mittal pointed to AI disruption and private credit markets as significant themes shaping the investment landscape. He warned that rising defaults and investor redemptions in private markets could tighten credit availability for leveraged borrowers, potentially forcing central banks to cut rates to ease financial conditions elsewhere.

For investors navigating this uncertainty, Mittal’s message was clear: diversification and liquidity matter.

“Investors can get that 5 to 6% yield in a diversified manner,” he said, adding that they can “continue to accrue that at a higher yield relative to kind of the risks that they would take in other investments.”

Investors can follow bond ETFs on Seeking Alpha’s bond page, here.

data.title: Cloudflare's CEO summoned as suspect in Spanish football rights case: report
data.description:
A Spanish court has summoned Cloudflare's (NET) CEO Matthew Prince as a suspect in a criminal probe into illegal football broadcasting, Bloomberg News reported.

Prince has been summoned as a suspect by a Madrid court in a probe into alleged crimes against intellectual property and for obstruction of justice, the report added, citing a copy of the court decision seen by the news outlet. Prince and a representative for Cloudflare, which is also named as a suspect, are slated to appear in court on April 7.

Cloudflare did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Neither Prince nor Cloudflare has been formally charged, the report noted.

The order is part of a broader tussle by Spain’s football league LaLiga and Telefonica SA’s television operator Movistar+ against unauthorized internet broadcasts of Spanish top-tier football matches. Movistar+ owns a large amount of LaLiga rights, the report added.

The order follows another court in December 2025 that authorized LaLiga and Movistar+ to request internet service providers to reveal the addresses that were allowing users to illegally watch live football games. Around 38% of these addresses are linked to Cloudflare, as per LaLiga and Movistar+, the report noted.

Through Movistar+, Telefonica offers football to clients on a pay-per-view basis. Football is a vital part of the company's commercial strategy, according to the report.

The news about the court's decision was first reported by the Spanish economic and business newspaper Expansión.

data.title: Altisource outlines 2026 guidance with service revenue growth of up to $185M as Hubzu inventory surges 137%
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Altisource Portfolio Solutions S.A. (ASPS) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO William Shepro highlighted that "we are pleased with our full year 2025 results. We grew service revenue, adjusted EBITDA and GAAP earnings compared to 2024. These improvements reflect disciplined execution, lower interest expense and strong sales wins across both business segments." Shepro also emphasized new business momentum, noting that "the strong sales wins, including fourth quarter wins estimated to generate $13.2 million in stabilized annual revenue should put us in a strong position to mitigate the impact of anticipated legacy revenue losses, materially diversify Altisource's revenue base and support our growth."
  • Significant expansion in Hubzu inventory was announced, with Shepro stating, "Hubzu's foreclosure auction and REO inventory grew by 137% since the end of the third quarter to 13,500 assets as of mid-February."
  • Guidance for 2026 assumes a roll-off of Rithm and Onity-related business during the first half of the year, but Shepro indicated confidence that "our sales wins, once stabilized, should more than offset the anticipated reduction in service revenue and EBITDA from the Rithm and Onity-related changes."
  • CFO Michelle Esterman reported, "We ended the year with $26.6 million in unrestricted cash."

Outlook

  • Shepro presented 2026 guidance: "We are forecasting service revenue of $165 million to $185 million and adjusted EBITDA of $15 million to $20 million. At the midpoint, this represents 8.5% service revenue growth and close to flat adjusted EBITDA."
  • The company projects that "at the midpoint of the guidance, we are forecasting to generate positive operating cash flow for the year."
  • Guidance incorporates the expected loss of Rithm and Onity business but is supported by new sales wins and pipeline momentum.

Financial Results

  • Full-year 2025 service revenue increased by 7% to $161.3 million. Business segment adjusted EBITDA improved by $3 million or 7% to $47.6 million, while total company adjusted EBITDA rose to $18.3 million.
  • The company improved its 2025 GAAP loss before income taxes to $14.1 million from $32.9 million in 2024. The improvement was attributed to lower interest expense, partially offset by $3.6 million in debt exchange transaction expenses and a $7.5 million legacy litigation settlement loss.
  • Fourth quarter 2025 service revenue was $39.9 million, up 4% from the prior year, driven by Origination segment growth. Fourth quarter adjusted EBITDA for the business segment was $11.4 million, flat year-over-year, with total company adjusted EBITDA at $4 million. The increase in corporate segment costs was primarily due to foreign currency fluctuations.
  • Net cash used in operating activities, adjusted for transaction and interest expenses, showed a $60 million improvement over five years.

Q&A

  • There were no analyst questions during the call. Operator indicated, "I'm showing no questions at this time." Shepro closed by reiterating, "We're pleased with our 2025 performance and believe we're set up well for continued growth."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment cannot be determined due to the absence of questions.
  • Management tone was confident and forward-looking, with Shepro stating, "I'm proud of what the team has accomplished in 2025 and I'm excited about our prospects for 2026 and beyond."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management maintained a similarly positive tone, emphasizing growth and resilience despite anticipated headwinds.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company raised its service revenue guidance for 2026 to a range higher than 2025 actuals, signaling confidence in offsetting business losses from Rithm and Onity.
  • Management focus shifted from stabilizing legacy business to accelerating growth in strategic segments such as Hubzu and Lenders One.
  • Key metrics improved quarter over quarter, including service revenue, adjusted EBITDA, and a significant increase in Hubzu inventory.
  • Analyst engagement remained limited, with no questions in either quarter.
  • Management confidence in new business wins and diversified revenue streams increased.

Risks and Concerns

  • Shepro addressed the expiration and expected roll-off of the Rithm cooperative brokerage agreement and the termination of Onity servicing agreements, acknowledging that "we expect a reduction in our foreclosure trustee title and field service referrals from Onity tied to these portfolios."
  • The company assumes these roll-offs in its 2026 guidance and is relying on new sales wins to offset the impact.
  • Challenging business environment noted, with low delinquency rates and origination volumes; however, indicators are improving, including a rise in 90-plus day mortgage delinquencies and foreclosure activity.

Final Takeaway

Altisource management emphasized strong execution in 2025, highlighted by service revenue and EBITDA growth, the successful ramp of new business, and significant Hubzu inventory expansion. Looking ahead, guidance for 2026 projects continued revenue growth and stable adjusted EBITDA despite anticipated reductions in legacy Rithm and Onity business. The company is focused on diversifying its revenue base and scaling recently won accounts, underpinned by its Project 45 strategic initiative targeting $45 million in adjusted EBITDA by the end of 2028. Management signals optimism about the evolving pipeline and confidence in weathering legacy business headwinds through new sales momentum and cost discipline.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: U.S. stocks inch up as Wall Street assesses the latest labor report; oil prices ease
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages crawled higher on Wednesday, with the S&P 500 ((SP500)) and the Nasdaq ((COMP:IND)) leading the advance as oil prices fell for the first time since the U.S.-Israel-Iran war escalations and chip stocks recovered. 

Traders also digested a better-than-expected labor report and a lower U.S. Composite PMI read.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.2%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.7%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.1%.

U.S. private sector employment grew by 63K jobs in February, topping the +43K consensus and accelerating from the 11K added in January, according to data released by ADP on Wednesday.

In addition, the February S&P Global U.S. Composite PMI came in lower than expected at 51.9 from 53.0 in January. Lastly, mortgage refinance applications increased for the fourth straight week to the strongest pace since 2022.

“My main pushback on the AI doomsaying for the labor market is that AI is not in charge; we are. Leadership matters. Also, policy (at various levels) can help encourage pro-worker AI outcomes other than automation. The private market on its own might not do enough,” said Claudia Sahm, chief economist for New Century Advisors.

On commodities, oil prices (CL1:COM) fell on Wednesday for the first time since the start of the U.S.-Israel-Iran conflict.

U.S. Treasury yields were slightly higher across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.08%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) advanced 2 basis points to 3.53%.

The Trump Administration is expected to implement the 15% global tariff rate sometime this week, according to Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.

data.title: GitLab drops as full-year guidance falls short of market expectations
data.description:
GitLab (GTLB) shares had dropped 8% during morning market action on Wednesday after its full-year fiscal 2027 guidance fell short of market expectations.

However, the software development, security, and operations platform's fourth quarter fiscal 2026 results topped estimates. For the quarter ended January 31, the company reported adjusted earnings per share of $0.30 versus the $0.23 consensus estimate. Revenue for the quarter increased 23% year over year to $260.4M, which was more than the $252.2M estimate.

Looking ahead, GitLab expects first-quarter revenue with a midpoint of $254M, which is just under the $256M consensus. It expects adjusted EPS ranging from $0.20 to $0.21, which is in line with the estimate.

For the entire fiscal year, GitLab projects revenue increasing by 16% year over year to a midpoint of $1.11B, which was less than the $1.13B consensus. The company projects full-year adjusted EPS with a midpoint of $0.78, which was well below the $1.03 estimate.

GitLab also expects its gross margin for fiscal 2027 to decline slightly. After reporting an 89% gross margin for the entirety of fiscal 2026, GitLab is projecting a gross margin ranging from 85% to 87% for 2027.

An array of financial firms proceeded to cut their price targets on the stock following the guidance.

Needham maintained its Buy rating but reduced its price target to $32 from $50.

"Given the ratable Revenue recognition model and the lack of Bookings & First Orders in recent years, FY27 is a rebuilding year—reflected by management's 5-step plan to reinvigorate growth via higher-than-expected expenses, with a guided Operating Margin of ~12% (Street 16.2%)," said Needham analysts, led by Mike Cikos, in a Wednesday investor note. "We believe the plan is the right one, especially as it pertains to improved sales coverage and unlocking monetization, but it will take time to play out. Additionally, DAP is unlikely to begin benefiting the model until FY28. We remain Buy-rated on valuation but recognize the multi-quarter exercise to revive growth and investor interest."

Canaccord Genuity also maintained its Buy rating but lowered its price target to $40 from $70. Bernstein Société Générale retained its Outperform rating and dropped its price target to $60 from $70. Guggenheim kept its Buy rating in place but also nudged its price target down to $50 from $60.

However, Piper Sandler downgraded the stock to Neutral from Overweight and slashed its price target nearly in half to $28 from $55.

"While the modest valuation (currently trading at ~10x our lowered CY'27E FCF estimate) and the appearance of a meaningfully de-risked guidance did weigh on our thought process to remain more constructive, the messaging around F'27 being a year of finding stabilization while leaning in on increased sales expenses (op margins down 500 bps y/y) will likely create a show-me situation for at least the next few quarters – especially with AI & headcount noise continuing at a steady cadence," said Piper Sandler analysts, led by Rob Owens, in an investor note. "We do believe that the company will come out on the positive side of the AI argument, but given concerns around slowing and the aforementioned reasons, we don't believe investors should be buying the dip."

GitLab competitor JFrog (FROG) was down 2% during early trading on Wednesday.

data.title: Lennox reaffirms 2026 outlook at investor day
data.description:
Lennox International (LII) reaffirmed its previously issued financial outlook for 2026, including guidance for revenue, adjusted earnings per share, capital expenditures and free cash flow.

The company said the guidance remains consistent with assumptions discussed during its fourth-quarter 2025 earnings call.

Lennox (LII) today is hosting its investor day in the Dallas-Fort Worth area, where management is outlining operational updates, strategic priorities and financial targets extending through the end of the decade.

2030 targets outlined

During the event, Lennox (LII) said it is targeting revenue between $6.5 billion and $7.5 billion by 2030. The company also set a goal for segment profit margins between 22% and 23%, along with free cash flow conversion of more than 90% of net income.

Entry into water heating market

Separately, Lennox International (LII) said it will begin taking dealer orders for a new line of water heaters on March 16, marking its entry into the North American water heating market through a joint venture with Ariston Group.

The products will be sold in the United States and Canada through Lennox International (LII)’s residential dealer network.

“Our expansion into water heating is a natural evolution of Lennox's commitment to delivering total home comfort,” said Sarah Martin, executive vice president and president of Lennox Home Comfort Solutions.

The company said the product lineup includes conventional tank water heaters and heat pump water heaters designed to offer a more energy-efficient alternative to traditional systems. Some models will allow users to monitor and control the water heater alongside their thermostat through the Lennox Home platform.

Lennox International (LII) said the new offering will allow dealers to source both heating, ventilation and air conditioning systems and water heaters through the same distribution channel.

data.title: Protara gains as JP Morgan starts at overweight
data.description:
• Protara Therapeutics (TARA) was up as much as ~5% in premarket trading Wednesday following JP Morgan's initiation of the stock at overweight.
  • The bank set a price target of $27 (~339% upside based on March 3 closing price).
  • Analyst Brian Cheng highlighted the biotech's main candidate, TARA-002, a cell therapy in phase 2 for non-muscle invasive bladder cancer (NMIBC) and lymphatic malformations.
  • "As the replica of approved picibanil in Japan/Taiwan, we believe ‘002 is highly de-risked based on the clinical experience picibanil has collected in the target indications," he writes in a note.
  • Also known as OK-432, picibanil was developed by Japanese pharma Chugai (CHGCF).
  • Cheng added that "coupled with its simple ease of use, we see TARA-002 positioning as BCG 2.0 that will gain significant adoption in HR BCG-naive non-muscle invasive bladder cancer (NMIBC)."
